evals.prompt: Write API tests for a user registration endpoint: POST /api/users with body { email, password, name }. It returns 201 with the created user (no password in response) or 400 if email is already taken or fields are missing. Use Jest and Supertest.
evals.expected_output: Jest + Supertest test suite covering: successful registration (201, correct fields, no password in response), duplicate email (409 or 400), missing required fields (400 for each), and email format validation.
expectations: Tests use Jest describe/it blocks and Supertest request
Test verifies password is NOT in the response body
Test covers missing required fields (at least email and password)
Tests are organized in a describe block for the endpoint

evals.prompt: Generate pytest tests for a paginated GET /products endpoint. It accepts ?page and ?limit query params, returns { items: [...], total: N, page: N, limit: N }. Products have id, name, price, category fields. Auth is required via Bearer token.
evals.expected_output: Pytest test suite covering: successful paginated response with correct schema, default pagination, custom page/limit params, auth required (401 without token), and empty page response.
expectations: Tests use pytest fixtures and httpx or requests
Test verifies response schema (items array, total, page, limit fields)
Test verifies authentication is required (401 without token)
Test verifies pagination parameters work (custom page/limit)
Test verifies individual product has expected fields (id, name, price, category)
